title: Recursion, Recurrences, and Divide-and-Conquer · Data-Structure Studio
summary: Break a large document and spatial search problem into independently checkable subproblems. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a recursive solver with recurrence evidence while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define base cases, progress measures, and combination rules before recursive calls.
Trace call trees, stack depth, repeated subproblems, and returned values.
Write and interpret recurrences for divide, solve, and combine costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
